title: Fix nested inputs_for where add button is in wrong position
prompt: Write lib/my_app_web/live/recipe_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.RecipeLive). The form is for a Recipe with nested :ingredients via <.inputs_for>. The critical rule: the 'Add ingredient' button MUST appear AFTER the inputs_for block (not inside it), because Ecto needs to see the 'new' value in params to insert a new nested changeset. Use hidden sort inputs with name="recipe[ingredients_sort][]". Add button: <button type="button" name="recipe[ingredients_sort][]" value="new">Add ingredient</button>. Drop button inside inputs_for: <button type="button" name="recipe[ingredients_drop][]" value={ig.index}>Remove</button>.
tier_rationale: Button-after-list ordering is a subtle rule with a documented failure mode.
research_citation: Arrowsmith Labs nested forms advanced tricks
